model_input:
You are a DVOC (Draft-Verify-Action Loop) verifier. A red crosshair shows the current predicted click position on the screen. A fixed blue circle provides spatial reference.

Call report_offset(dx, dy, confidence) with the pixel offset from the crosshair to the target element center.
dx: pixels right (negative = left). dy: pixels down (negative = up).
confidence: 0.0 (uncertain) to 1.0 (certain).

Only report dx=0, dy=0 when the crosshair is within a few pixels of the target. If uncertain, set lower confidence so the loop keeps refining.

Task: Click the Profile link in the dropdown menu
Previous iterations:
  iter 0: at (361,205) said dx=220 dy=209 conf=1.00 -> (581,414)
  iter 1: at (581,414) said dx=40 dy=-55 conf=0.90 -> (621,359)
  iter 2: at (601,386) said dx=0 dy=-34 conf=0.90 -> (601,352)
  iter 3: at (601,378) said dx=-100 dy=-18 conf=0.90 -> (501,360)
  iter 4: at (576,374) said dx=40 dy=0 conf=0.90 -> (616,374)
  iter 5: at (586,374) said dx=32 dy=-21 conf=0.90 -> (618,352)
Call report_offset().

raw_message.reasoning_content:
The user wants me to click the 'Profile' link in the dropdown menu.
The current crosshair is at (594, 368) - Wait, the image says (594, 368) in the bottom right, let's double check.
Actually, looking at the image:
The red crosshair is over the user icon next to 'Profile'.
The 'Profile' link is the text "Profile" and its icon.
The crosshair is currently at (594, 368) according to the corner text (though it might be just a label).
Let's look at the image.
The center of the "Profile" link (including icon and text) is roughly where the crosshair is.
The crosshair is centered on the user icon.
The target is the 'Profile' link.
The text 'Profile' starts to the right of the icon.
Usually, clicking the icon or the text works.
The center of the "Profile" row (icon + text) is slightly to the right of where the crosshair is.
Crosshair is at roughly (465, 507) in normalized coords? No, the screen is likely 1000x1000 or something else.
Let's use the coordinates provided in the previous iterations to get a sense of scale.
Iter 5: at (586, 374) said dx=32 dy=-21 -> (618, 353).
Current image shows the crosshair. It's on the icon.
The "Profile" text is to the right.
The center of the "Profile" button (icon + text) would be slightly to the right.
Wait, the red crosshair is practically on the icon.
Let's estimate the offset to the center of the "Profile" row.
Icon is at approx (465, 507) in image pixels if 1000x1000?
Let's look at the image again.
Crosshair is at the icon.
'Profile' text is to the right.
Center of 'Profile' row is roughly halfway between the left edge of the icon and the right edge of the text.
The crosshair is on the icon.
I need to move it right to center it on the whole link.
Actually, the crosshair is very close to the center of the icon.
Let's see:
Crosshair x is around 465 (normalized).
Text 'Profile' extends to around 540.
Center of (440, 540) is 490.
Offset dx = 490 - 465 = 25.
Let's check y.
Crosshair is on the icon. Icon center is y ~ 507.
The text 'Profile' is also centered at y ~ 507.
So dy = 0.
Wait, the bottom right corner says (594, 368).
If the crosshair is at (594, 368), and the target center is around (620, 368).
Then dx = 26, dy = 0.
Let's look at the image.
The crosshair is centered on the person icon.
The text 'Profile' is to the right.
The overall "Profile" link area is the rectangle containing both.
The center of that rectangle is further right.
dx is about 30-40 pixels.
dy is about 0.
Looking at Iter 5: at (586, 374) said dx=32 dy=-21 -> (618, 353).
Currently the crosshair is at (594, 368) according to the tag.
The center of 'Profile' text + icon is roughly (620, 368).
dx = 620 - 594 = 26.
dy = 368 - 368 = 0.
Let's refine.
The crosshair is slightly above the horizontal center of the text? No, it looks centered vertically.
Let's say dx=30, dy=0. Confidence 0.9.
